name: Hierarchical (Agglomerative) Clustering
applicable_scenarios: build a nested hierarchy (dendrogram) of clusters without fixing k up front
explore cluster structure at multiple granularities
small-to-medium datasets where a full distance matrix fits in memory
math_form: d(A, B) = \min/\max/\text{avg}\{d(a,b) : a \in A, b \in B\}
common_pitfalls: O(n^2) memory for the distance matrix; doesn't scale beyond ~50k points
ward linkage implicitly assumes Euclidean distance and spherical clusters
linkage choice changes results dramatically; single linkage chains noisy clusters
dendrogram cut height is subjective; justify k via silhouette
